## Description

> Provide a concise summary of the change and the motivation behind it. Link to the relevant Issue below.

**Resolves:** #

---

## Type of Change

> Select all that apply.

- [ ] `feat`: New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] `fix`: Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] `refactor`: Code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- [ ] `perf`: Performance improvement
- [ ] `chore`: Maintenance, configuration, dependency updates
- [ ] `docs`: Documentation changes only
- [ ] `test`: Adding or updating tests
- [ ] `BREAKING CHANGE`: Fix or feature that changes existing functionality

---

- `app/templates/example.html` — Updated Jinja template to render Y.
- `config.py` — Added `NEW_CONFIG_VAR` for Z.

## Database Impact

> **Reminder:** This project uses raw `psycopg2`. All queries must be parameterized (`%s` placeholders). No ORM queries.

### AI-Generated Artifacts

| File / Function | AI Contribution Level | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `app/services/example.py` | Full generation | Reviewed and approved as-is |
| `app/templates/example.html` | Partial generation | Logic corrected by developer |

### Human Verification Steps
> Document exactly how you verified the AI-generated code was correct, safe, and production-ready.

- [ ] Read through every line of AI-generated code before committing.
- [ ] Confirmed all SQL queries use `%s` parameterization (no f-string or `.format()` injection risks).
- [ ] Tested all generated service functions manually against a live DB.
- [ ] Reviewed for logic errors that the AI may have introduced (e.g., off-by-one, wrong FK reference).
- [ ] Cross-referenced against existing code patterns (e.g., `auth_service.py`) to ensure consistency.

### Known Limitations / Deviations from AI Output
> Note anything the AI got wrong, or was corrected/changed before merging.

```
[e.g., "AI used string formatting in the SQL query; corrected to parameterized %s."]
[e.g., "AI suggested SQLAlchemy ORM — overridden to use raw psycopg2 per project standards."]
```

## Checklist

- [ ] My code follows the project's existing style and conventions.
- [ ] I have reviewed my own code thoroughly (diff review).
- [ ] I have added comments to complex or non-obvious code blocks.
- [ ] Any new environment variables are documented in `.env.example`.
- [ ] I have updated the `README.md` if functionality has changed.
- [ ] The PR targets the correct base branch (not `main` directly for feature work).
